author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

On the words of the Gospel, Luke xii. 35, ‘Let your loins be girded about, and your lamps burning; and be ye yourselves like,’ etc. And on the words of the 34th Psalm, v. 12, ‘what man is he that desireth life,’ etc.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, Luke xii. 35, ‘Let your loins be girded about, and your lamps burning; and be ye yourselves like,’ etc. And on the words of the 34th Psalm, v. 12, ‘what man is he that desireth life,’ etc.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Our Lord Jesus Christ both came to men, and went away from men, and is to come to men.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And yet He was here when He came, nor did He depart when He went away, and He is to come to them to whom He said, “Lo, I am with you, even unto the end of the world.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: According to the “form of a servant” then, which He took for our sakes, was He born at a certain time, and was slain, and rose again, and now “dieth no more, neither shall death have any more dominion over Him;” but according to His Divinity, wherein He was equal to the Father, was He already in this world, and “the world was made by Him, and the world knew Him not.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: On this point ye have just heard the Gospel, what admonition it has given us, putting us on our guard, and wishing us to be unencumbered and prepared to await the end; that after these last things, which are to be feared in this world, that rest may succeed which hath no end.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Blessed are they who shall be partakers of it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For then shall they be in security, who are not in security now; and again then shall they fear, who will not fear now.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Unto this waiting, and for this hope’s sake, have we been made Christians.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Is not our hope not of this world?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us then not love the world.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: From the love of this world have we been called away, that we may hope for and love another.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In this world ought we to abstain from all unlawful desires, to have, that is, “our loins girded;” and to be fervent and to shine in good works, that is, to have “our lights burning.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the Lord Himself said to His disciples in another place of the Gospel, “No man lighteth a candle and putteth it under a bushel, but on a candlestick, that it may give light unto all that are in the house.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And to show of what He was speaking, He subjoined and said, “Let your light so shine before men, that they may see your good works, and glorify your Father which is in heaven.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore He would that “our loins should be girded, and our lights burning.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “our loins girded”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Depart from evil.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is to “burn”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is to have our “lights burning”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is this, “And do good.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is that which He said afterwards, “And ye yourselves like unto men that wait for their Lord, when He will return from the wedding:” except that which follows in that Psalm, “Seek after peace, and ensue it”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: These three things, that is, “abstaining from evil, and doing good,” and the hope of everlasting reward, are recorded in the Acts of the Apostles, where it is written, that Paul taught them of “temperance and righteousness,” and the hope of eternal life.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To temperance belongs, “let your loins be girded.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To righteousness, “and your lights burning.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To the hope of eternal life, the waiting for the Lord.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then, “depart from evil,” this is temperance, these are the loins girded: “and do good,” this is righteousness, these are the “lights burning;” “seek peace, and ensue it,” this is the waiting for the world to come: therefore, “Be ye like unto men that wait for their Lord, when He will come from the wedding.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Having then these precepts and promises, why seek we on earth for “good days,” where we cannot find them?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For I know that ye do seek them, when ye are either sick, or in any of the tribulations, which in this world abound.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For when life draws towards its close, the old man is full of complaints, and with no joys.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Amid all the tribulations by which mankind is worn away, men seek for nothing but “good days,” and wish for a long life, which here they cannot have.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For even a man’s long life is narrowed within so short a span to the wide extent of all ages, as if it were but one drop to the whole sea.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then is man’s life, even that which is called a long one?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They call that a long life, which even in this world’s course is short; and as I have said, groans abound even unto the decrepitude of old age.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This at the most is but brief, and of short duration; and yet how eagerly is it sought by men, with how great diligence, with how great toil, with how great carefulness, with how great watchfulness, with how great labour do men seek to live here for a long time, and to grow old.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And yet this very living long, what is it but running to the end?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hadst yesterday, and thou dost wish also to have to-morrow.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But when this day and to-morrow are passed, thou hast them not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore thou dost wish for the day to break, that that may draw near to thee whither thou hast no wish to come.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou makest some annual festival with thy friends, and hearest it there said to thee by thy well-wishers, “Mayest thou live many years,” thou dost wish that what they have said, may come to pass.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Dost thou wish that years and years may come, and the end of these years come not?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thy wishes are contrary to one another; thou dost wish to walk on, and dost not wish to reach the end.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if, as I have said, there is so great care in men, as to desire with daily, great and perpetual labours, to die somewhat later: with how great cause ought they to strive, that they may never die?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Of this, no one will think.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Day by day “good days” are sought for in this world, where they are not found; yet no one wishes so to live, that he may arrive there where they are found.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore the same Scripture admonishes us, and says, “Who is the man that wisheth for life, and loveth to see good days?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Scripture so asked the question, as that It knew well what answer would be given It; knowing that all men would “seek for life and good days.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In accordance with their desire It asked the question, as if the answer would be given It from the heart of all, “I wish it;” It said thus, “Who is the man that wisheth for life, and loveth to see good days?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Just as even at this very hour in which I am speaking to you, when ye heard me say, “Who is the man that wisheth for life, and loveth to see good days?” ye all answered in your heart, “I.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For so do I too, who am speaking with you, “wish for life and good days;” what ye seek, that do I seek also.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Just as if gold were necessary for us all, and we all, I as well as you, were wishing to get at the gold, and there was some anywhere in a field of yours, in a place subject to your power, and I were to see you searching for it, and were to say to you, “What are ye searching for?” ye were to answer me, “Gold.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And I were to say to you, “Ye are searching for gold, and I am searching for gold too: what ye are searching for, I am searching for; but ye are not searching for it where we can find it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Listen to me then, where we can find it; I am not taking it away from you, I am showing you the spot;” yea, let us all follow Him, who knows where what we are seeking for, is.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So now too seeing that ye desire “life and good days,” we cannot say to you, “Do not desire ‘life and good days;’” but this we say, “Do not seek for ‘life and good days’ here in this world, where ‘good days’ cannot be.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Is not this life itself like unto death?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now these days here hasten and pass away: for to-day has shut out yesterday; tomorrow only rises that it may shut out to-day.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: These days themselves have no abiding; wherefore wouldest thou abide with them?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Your desire then whereby ye wish for “life and good days,” I not only do not repress, but I even more strongly inflame.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By all means “seek” for “life, seek for good days;” but let them be sought there, where they can be found.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For would ye with me hear His counsel, who knoweth where “good days” and where “life” is?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear it not from me, but together with me.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For One says to us, “Come, ye children, hearken unto Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And let us run together, and stand, and prick up our ears, and with our hearts understand the Father, who hath said, “Come, ye children, hearken unto Me, I will teach you the fear of the Lord.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And then follows what he would teach us, and to what end the fear of the Lord is useful.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Who is the man that wisheth life, and loveth to see good days?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We all answer, “We wish it.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us listen then to what follows, “Refrain thy tongue from evil, and thy lips that they speak no guile.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now say, “I wish it.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Just now when I said, “Who is the man that wisheth for life, and loveth to see good days?” we all answered, “I.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Come then, let some one now answer “I.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then, “Refrain thy tongue from evil, and thy lips that they speak no guile.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now say, “I.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wouldest thou then have “good days” and “life,” and wouldest thou not “refrain thy tongue from evil, and thy lips that they speak no guile”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Alert to the reward, slow to the work!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And to whom if he does not work is the reward rendered?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I would that in thy house thou wouldest render the reward even to him that does work!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For to him that works not, I am sure thou dost not render it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because thou owest nothing to him that does not work!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And God hath a reward proposed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What reward?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Life and good days,” which life we all desire, and unto which days we all strive to come.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The promised reward He will give us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What reward?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Life and good days.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what are “good days”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Life without end, rest without labour.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Great is the reward He hath set before us: in so great a reward as is set before us, let us see what He hath commanded us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For enkindled by the reward of so great a promise, and by the love of the reward, let us make ready at once our strength, our sides, our arms, to do His bidding.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Is it as if He were to command us to carry heavy burdens, to dig something it may be, or to raise up some machine?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: No, no such laborious thing hath He enjoined thee, but hath enjoined thee only to “refrain” that member which amongst all thy members thou dost move so quickly.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Refrain thy tongue from evil.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is no labour to erect a building, and is it a labour to hold in the tongue?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Refrain thy tongue from evil.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Speak no lie, speak no revilings, speak no slanders, speak no false witnesses, speak no blasphemies.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Refrain thy tongue from evil.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See how angry thou art, if any one speaks evil of thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As thou art angry with another, when he speaks evil of thee; so be thou angry with thyself, when thou speakest evil of another.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Let thy lips speak no guile.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is in thine heart within, be that spoken out.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let not thy breast conceal one thing, and thy tongue utter another.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Depart from evil, and do good.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For how should I say, “Clothe the naked,” to him who up to this time would strip him that is clothed?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For he that oppresses his fellow-citizen, how can he take in the stranger?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then in proper order, first “depart from evil,” and “do good;” first “gird up thy loins,” and then “light the lamp.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And when thou hast done this, wait in assured hope for “life and good days.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Seek peace, and ensue it;” and then with a good face wilt thou say unto the Lord, “I have done what Thou hast bidden, render me what Thou hast promised.”
